nodes.label: :mod:`websockets.exceptions` defines the following hierarchy of exceptions.  * :
Base class for all exceptions defined by websockets.
Raised when trying to interact with a closed connection.      Attributes:
Like :exc:`ConnectionClosed`, when the connection terminated properly.      A cl
Like :exc:`ConnectionClosed`, when the connection terminated with an error.
Raised when connecting to a URI that isn't a valid WebSocket URI.
Raised when connecting via a proxy that isn't valid.
Base class for exceptions raised when the opening handshake fails.
Raised when a handshake request or response breaks a security rule.      Securit
Raised when failing to connect to a proxy.
Raised when an HTTP proxy response is malformed.
Raised when an HTTP proxy rejects the connection.
Raised when a handshake request or response is malformed.
Raised when a handshake response rejects the WebSocket upgrade.
Raised when an HTTP header doesn't have a valid format or value.
Raised when an HTTP header cannot be parsed.      The format of the header doesn
Raised when an HTTP header has a wrong value.      The format of the header is c
Raised when the Origin header in a request isn't allowed.
Raised when the Upgrade or Connection header isn't correct.
Raised when negotiating an extension or a subprotocol fails.
Raised when a parameter name is repeated in an extension header.
Raised when a parameter name in an extension header is invalid.
Raised when a parameter value in an extension header is invalid.
Raised when receiving or sending a frame that breaks the protocol.      The Sans
Raised when parsing a frame with a payload that exceeds the maximum size.      T
Raised when sending a frame is forbidden in the current state.      Specifically
Raised when receiving or sending messages concurrently.      WebSocket is a conn
